arguments.content:
---
created_at: 2026-04-21T15:24:00+08:00
author: Alex Zhang
status: active
guide_effective_from: 2026-04-21T00:00:00+08:00
---

# Project Orion — New Team Member Onboarding Guide

**Version:** 1.0  
**Last Updated:** April 21, 2026  
**Guide Effective From:** April 21, 2026

---

Welcome to Project Orion! This guide will help you get up and running during your first week on the team.

---

## Environment Setup

### 1. Development Machine

**Required Specifications:**
- macOS 13+ or Ubuntu 22.04 LTS
- 16GB RAM minimum (32GB recommended)
- 50GB free disk space

**Essential Software:**
- [ ] **Git** (v2.40+)
  ```bash
  git --version
  ```
- [ ] **Docker Desktop** (v4.20+)
  ```bash
  docker --version
  docker-compose --version
  ```
- [ ] **Python** (v3.11.4)
  ```bash
  pyenv install 3.11.4
  pyenv global 3.11.4
  ```
- [ ] **Node.js** (v18.16+ LTS)
  ```bash
  nvm install 18
  nvm use 18
  ```
- [ ] **PostgreSQL Client** (psql v14+)
  ```bash
  brew install libpq  # macOS
  # or
  sudo apt-get install postgresql-client  # Ubuntu
  ```

### 2. Repository Setup

```bash
# Clone the main repository
git clone git@github.com:oriontech/project-orion.git
cd project-orion

# Set up Python environment
python -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt
pip install -r requirements-dev.txt

# Install pre-commit hooks
pre-commit install

# Copy environment configuration
cp .env.example .env
# Ask your buddy for the local dev secrets

# Start local infrastructure
docker-compose up -d db redis

# Run database migrations
alembic upgrade head

# Seed local database with sample data
python scripts/seed_dev_data.py

# Start the API server
uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000
```

Verify setup: http://localhost:8000/health should return `{"status":"ok"}`

### 3. Frontend Setup (if applicable)

```bash
cd frontend
npm install
npm run dev
```

Verify: http://localhost:3000 loads the Orion dashboard

---

## Key Tools

### Development Tools

| Tool | Purpose | Access | Documentation |
|------|---------|--------|---------------|
| **GitHub** | Code repository, PRs | https://github.com/oriontech | Internal wiki |
| **Slack** | Team communication | #project-orion, #orion-dev | Onboarding channel |
| **Linear** | Issue tracking | https://linear.app/orion | [Guide](https://wiki/linear-guide) |
| **Figma** | Design mockups | Design team invite | [Figma 101](https://wiki/figma) |
| **Datadog** | Monitoring, logs | SSO login | [Runbook](https://wiki/monitoring) |
| **AWS Console** | Infrastructure | Request access from Alex | [AWS Policy](https://wiki/aws) |

### Internal Services

| Service | URL | Purpose |
|---------|-----|---------|
| **Staging API** | https://api-staging.project-orion.com | Testing environment |
| **Staging App** | https://staging.project-orion.com | QA environment |
| **Internal Wiki** | https://wiki.oriontech.io | Documentation hub |
| **API Docs** | https://docs.project-orion.com | Public API reference |

### Development Workflow Tools

- **pre-commit**: Automated code formatting and linting
- **pytest**: Test runner (`pytest -xvs` for verbose)
- **black**: Python code formatter
- **ruff**: Fast Python linter
- **mypy**: Static type checking

---

## Workflow Overview

### Sprint Cycle (2 weeks)

```
Week 1                          Week 2
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Mon: Sprint Planning            Mon: Development continues
Tue-Thu: Development            Tue-Wed: Feature complete
Fri: Mid-sprint review          Thu: Code freeze, QA
                                Fri: Sprint demo + Retro
```

### Daily Workflow

1. **Morning standup** (9:30 AM CST) — Slack #orion-standup
2. **Pull latest code**: `git pull origin main`
3. **Check Linear**: Review assigned issues
4. **Create feature branch**: `git checkout -b feature/OR-123-short-desc`
5. **Development**: Write code + tests
6. **Pre-commit checks**: Run automatically on commit
7. **Push and create PR**: Use Linear issue ID in PR title
8. **Code review**: 2 approvals required for backend, 1 for frontend
9. **Merge**: Squash and merge after CI passes

### Git Branch Naming Conventions

| Prefix | Use Case | Example |
|--------|----------|---------|
| `feature/` | New features | `feature/OR-456-json-export` |
| `bugfix/` | Bug fixes | `bugfix/OR-789-timeout-fix` |
| `hotfix/` | Production fixes | `hotfix/fix-login-redirect` |
| `docs/` | Documentation | `docs/update-api-reference` |

### Pull Request Workflow

1. Fill out PR template (linked automatically)
2. Link Linear issue: `Closes OR-123`
3. Add screenshots for UI changes
4. Ensure CI checks pass
5. Request review from:
   - Backend code → Alex or Bai
   - Frontend code → Chen
   - Database changes → Alex
6. Address review comments
7. Merge when approved

---

## First-Week Checklist

### Day 1 — Welcome & Setup

- [ ] Complete HR onboarding (badge, laptop, accounts)
- [ ] Meet your onboarding buddy (assigned by Alex)
- [ ] Get added to GitHub organization (oriontech)
- [ ] Join Slack channels: #project-orion, #orion-dev, #orion-standup
- [ ] Get Linear access and walkthrough
- [ ] Set up local development environment
- [ ] Complete repository clone and verify API starts

### Day 2 — First Contribution

- [ ] Attend standup (observe and introduce yourself)
- [ ] Pick a "good first issue" from Linear (label: `onboarding`)
- [ ] Create your first feature branch
- [ ] Make a small change (fix typo, add docstring, etc.)
- [ ] Open your first PR
- [ ] Get code review feedback

### Day 3 — Understanding the System

- [ ] Read [Project Overview](https://wiki/project-overview) on internal wiki
- [ ] Review [API Reference](https://docs.project-orion.com)
- [ ] Explore the database schema (`app/models/`)
- [ ] Run the test suite: `pytest -x`
- [ ] Pair program with your buddy on a feature

### Day 4 — Production Awareness

- [ ] Read [On-Call Runbook](https://wiki/on-call) (observe only)
- [ ] Explore Datadog dashboards (staging)
- [ ] Shadow on-call engineer for 30 minutes
- [ ] Review recent incident post-mortems
- [ ] Understand deployment process (staging only)

### Day 5 — First Week Wrap-Up

- [ ] Complete 1-2 merged PRs (small tasks)
- [ ] Schedule 1:1 with Alex Zhang (calendar link in slack bio)
- [ ] Fill out first-week feedback form (HR will send)
- [ ] Join team lunch (Fridays at 12:30 PM)
- [ ] Set goals for Week 2 with your buddy

### Ongoing First Month

- [ ] Complete security training (week 2)
- [ ] Attend architecture overview session (week 2)
- [ ] Lead your first standup update (week 3)
- [ ] Deploy to production (with supervision, week 3-4)
- [ ] Complete 30-day check-in with Alex

---

## Team Contacts

| Role | Name | Slack | Responsibilities |
|------|------|-------|------------------|
| Tech Lead (Backend) | Alex Zhang | @alex.zhang | Architecture, backend PRs, deployments |
| Backend Engineer | Bai | @bai | API development, database design |
| Frontend Engineer | Chen | @chen | React/TypeScript, UI components |
| QA Engineer | Dana | @dana | Testing, bug triage, release validation |
| Product Manager | TBD | @pm-orion | Requirements, roadmap, customer feedback |

---

## Recommended Reading

1. **Project Documentation**
   - [Project Overview](https://wiki/project-overview)
   - [API Architecture](https://wiki/api-architecture)
   - [Database Schema](https://wiki/db-schema)

2. **Code Conventions**
   - [Python Style Guide](https://wiki/python-style)
   - [API Design Guidelines](https://wiki/api-design)
   - [Testing Best Practices](https://wiki/testing)

3. **Sprint Context**
   - Latest Sprint Kickoff notes: `day27/sprint10_kickoff.md`
   - Current ADRs in `docs/architecture/`

---

## Getting Help

**Stuck on setup?**
→ Ping your onboarding buddy or post in #orion-dev

**Questions about code or architecture?**
→ Ask in #orion-dev or schedule time with Alex

**Found a bug?**
→ Create a Linear issue with label `bug` and reproduction steps

**Have a suggestion?**
→ We love feedback! Mention it in retro or DM Alex

---

## Quick Reference Commands

```bash
# Start/stop local infrastructure
docker-compose up -d db redis
docker-compose down

# Run tests
pytest                              # All tests
pytest -xvs tests/unit/test_export.py  # Specific file
pytest -k test_json_export         # Filter by name

# Database migrations
alembic revision --autogenerate -m "Add exports table"
alembic upgrade head
alembic downgrade -1

# Code quality
black app/ tests/                  # Format
ruff check app/                    # Lint
mypy app/                          # Type check
pre-commit run --all-files         # Pre-commit checks

# Redis CLI (inside Docker)
docker-compose exec redis redis-cli

# Database CLI
docker-compose exec db psql -U orion -d orion_dev
```

---

Welcome to the team! We're excited to have you building Project Orion with us. 🚀

---

*Questions or suggestions for this guide? Open a PR or DM Alex Zhang.*
